content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 18, 1991

Honorable Walter R. Tucker
205 South Willowbrook Ave.
Compton, CA 90220

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-418

Dear Mr. Tucker:

This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
and confirms the telephone advice provided to you on August 27,
1991, regarding the permissible uses of campaign funds under the
provisions of the Campaign Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Under the provisions of the Act, is it permissible to use
campaign funds for the purpose of enlisting community support for
redefining the position of mayor as a compensated full time
position?

CONCLUSION

Enlisting community support to make the position of mayor a
compensated full time position is an activity which is directly
related to a political, governmental, or legislative purpose.
Thus, it is permissible to use campaign funds to defray the cost
of such an activity.

FACTS

You are the mayor of the City of Compton. The position of
mayor is, at this time, an uncompensated part time position. You
wish to use campaign funds to fund a public relations campaign to

_____________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. The Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-91-418
Page 2

disseminate information and gain community support for making the
position of mayor a full time compensated position.

ANALYSIS

The general rule is that any expenditure of campaign funds
must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose associated with a candidate's
office. (Section 89512.) Furthermore, where an expenditure
confers a substantial personal benefit on the candidate or
officeholder, the expenditure must be directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose. A substantial
personal benefit is defined as a direct personal benefit to the
candidate or officeholder of $100 or more. (Section 89511(b)(2).)

The use of campaign funds for professional services is, by
statute, permissible only if directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose. Section 89513(b) provides
that:

     (b) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay
for or reimburse the cost of professional services
unless the services are directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

     (1) Expenditures by a campaign committee to
pay for professional services reasonably required
by the campaign committee to assist it in the
performance of its administrative functions are
directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.

     You propose to engage the services of a public relations firm
to enhance community awareness for the need to make the office of
the mayor a full time compensated position. This activity is
directly related to a legislative and governmental purpose and
thus an expenditure of campaign funds for this purpose is
permissible. An expenditure of campaign funds is directly related
to a political purpose when such expenditure is necessary to the
quest for elective office and is incurred in connection with
programs or events to inform voters as to the current position of
a candidate on issues relevant to his or her campaign for elective
office.2 Your proposed activities will enhance your visibility in
the community and inform voters of your position regarding the
office of the mayor. Accordingly, the proposed activities are
directly related to a political purpose and expenditures of
campaign funds for these purposes is not prohibited under the Act.

___________________
2  The Commission is now in the process of enacting regulations
which will clarify the provisions of Section 89512. (See Proposed
Regulation 18952.)

Our File No. A-91-418
Page 3

Your state in your request for confirmation of the telephone
advice provided to you that you will not seek reelection to the
office of mayor unless the office of the mayor becomes a
compensated full time position. Presumably, if the position were
full time, your compensation would exceed $100, thus providing you
with a direct personal benefit. Under this set of circumstances,
any use of campaign funds must be directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose and not just reasonably
related. However, as discussed above, the activities in which you
propose to engage are directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose and thus use of campaign
funds for such purposes is permissible even though, if you are
successful, the activities may result in a personal benefit to you
in that, if the position of mayor becomes full time and if you are
reelected to the position, you will receive a direct personal
benefit in the form of compensation in excess of $100.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.³

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Bianca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division




3 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.analysis:
The general rule is that any expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose associated with a candidate's office. (Section 89512.) Furthermore, where an expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on the candidate or officeholder, the expenditure must be directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. A substantial personal benefit is defined as a direct personal benefit to the candidate or officeholder of $100 or more. (Section 89511(b)(2).)

The use of campaign funds for professional services is, by statute, permissible only if directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. Section 89513(b) provides that:

(b) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay for or reimburse the cost of professional services unless the services are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

(1) Expenditures by a campaign committee to pay for professional services reasonably required by the campaign committee to assist it in the performance of its administrative functions are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

You propose to engage the services of a public relations firm to enhance community awareness for the need to make the office of the mayor a full time compensated position. This activity is directly related to a legislative and governmental purpose and thus an expenditure of campaign funds for this purpose is permissible. An expenditure of campaign funds is directly related to a political purpose when such expenditure is necessary to the quest for elective office and is incurred in connection with programs or events to inform voters as to the current position of a candidate on issues relevant to his or her campaign for elective office.2 Your proposed activities will enhance your visibility in the community and inform voters of your position regarding the office of the mayor. Accordingly, the proposed activities are directly related to a political purpose and expenditures of campaign funds for these purposes is not prohibited under the Act.

2 The Commission is now in the process of enacting regulations which will clarify the provisions of Section 89512. (See Proposed Regulation 18952.)

Your state in your request for confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you that you will not seek reelection to the office of mayor unless the office of the mayor becomes a compensated full time position. Presumably, if the position were full time, your compensation would exceed $100, thus providing you with a direct personal benefit. Under this set of circumstances, any use of campaign funds must be directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose and not just reasonably related. However, as discussed above, the activities in which you propose to engage are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose and thus use of campaign funds for such purposes is permissible even though, if you are successful, the activities may result in a personal benefit to you in that, if the position of mayor becomes full time and if you are reelected to the position, you will receive a direct personal benefit in the form of compensation in excess of $100.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry. Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.3

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under the provisions of the Act, is it permissible to use campaign funds for the purpose of enlisting community support for redefining the position of mayor as a compensated full time position?

CONCLUSION: Enlisting community support to make the position of mayor a compensated full time position is an activity which is directly related to a political, governmental, or legislative purpose. Thus, it is permissible to use campaign funds to defray the cost of such an activity.
